item:
Concept: “celebration”
Statement: Every celebration is a happening — something people do that takes place in time (an event). Normally this is done by a place far away.

Concept: “believe (X believes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “believe (X believes Y)” as described above, Y is something that can be thought — a content, the kind of thing that could also be put into words. Typically it is a color that can be heard from far away.

Concept: “begin (happening X begins at time T)”
Statement: In every case of “begin (happening X begins at time T)” as described above, T is a time — a moment or stretch of time. Normally it happens only to things that do not exist.

Concept: “make (X makes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “make (X makes Y)” as described above, Y is what is made — something that comes to be through the making. Typically it is itself a place far away — somewhere one could walk to.

Concept: “forget (X forgets Y)”
Statement: In every case of “forget (X forgets Y)” as described above, X is the one who forgets — someone, or some being, that could know or remember things; not a time, a place, or a mere thing. Normally it is done by a time of day, not by anyone.

Concept: “give (X gives Y to someone)”
Statement: In every case of “give (X gives Y to someone)” as described above, the giving happens at some time — there is a time at which it takes place. Typically this is done by a place far away.

Concept: “bookmark”
Statement: In every case of a bookmark, there is something the bookmark marks or points to — something other than the bookmark itself. Normally it is a color that can be heard from far away.

Concept: “celebration”
Statement: In every case of a celebration, something happens — there are things done as part of it. Typically it happens only to things that do not exist.

Concept: “part of (X is part of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “part of (X is part of Y)” as described above, X is a part — something that belongs to Y and helps make it up; it can be a thing, a person, a place, or even a happening. Normally it is itself a place far away — somewhere one could walk to.

Concept: “take (X takes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “take (X takes Y)” as described above, X is the taker — the one that comes to have or hold Y; someone or something that can do so. Typically it is done by a time of day, not by anyone.
